summary: Test suite provides basic structural validation and two end-to-end integration tests via act, covering happy paths (COMPLIANT all-approved dependencies) and a single failure path (NON_COMPLIANT mixed licenses). However, critical gaps exist: (1) Only 2 functional test cases via act—insufficient for regression confidence given task complexity; (2) No validation of the primary requirement to 'handle errors gracefully'—missing tests for malformed manifests, missing files, corrupt license databases, unsupported formats; (3) No testing of edge cases: empty manifests, dependencies without versions, special characters in names, very large manifests, invalid license formats; (4) The requirement 'Mock the license lookup for testing' is not explicitly validated—tests assume fixtures exist but don't verify mock behavior; (5) Test design is brittle, relying on exact string matching in act output format, which may break with act version changes; (6) No unit-level testing of core parsing/checking logic, only integration tests; (7) test_helper functions (make_test_repo, run_act_push, record_case) and fixture content not provided, so test infrastructure cannot be fully assessed. Workflow structure tests are well-organized and validate required CI/CD elements (triggers, checkout, actionlint, shellcheck), but functional test coverage is severely limited.
